rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PONGRACZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To First Amended Objections Of Objectors Debra Duncan And Nicole Biddinger To Petition For Probate Of Will
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's demurrer to objections is sustained without leave to amend. Objectors are neither beneficiaries of decedent's will nor potential intestate heirs. Objectors' interest in property claimed by the estate will be determined in the separate trust proceeding. If it is determined that the property belongs to the trust estate, there will be nothing for the decedent's estate to administer or distribute, and the admission of decedent's will would not have affected Objectors' rights. If some or all of the property is determined to belong to decedent's estate, Objectors will have no rights with respect to that property, and the management and distribution of any such property will not affect Objectors' interests. Any evidentiary effect of decedent's intention as to the relationahip of the will to the trust will be fully resolved in deciding the 850 petition. In short, while it may be prudent to put off deciding the probate petition until the 850 petition is resolved since there will be no estate to administer unless some portion of the property is found to belong to decedent's estate, the outcome of the probate petition can have no effect on either the outcome of the 850 petition or on Objectors' rights. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, May 31, 2017.
